exam_format: Computer-based (tablet interface); at home via ProctorU or at Prometric test centers. 3 scored sections of approximately 23-27 questions each (~35 min per section) plus one unscored variable section and an unscored Writing sample (taken separately). Note: As of August 2024, the Analytical Reasoning (Logic Games) section has been replaced by a second Reading Comprehension section per updated LSAC test design.
exam_price_notes: $200 USD standard registration; $215 USD late registration. Fee waivers available for eligible candidates via lsac.org.

testing_centers.notes: LSAT available at home via ProctorU online proctoring. Must have a reliable internet connection, webcam, and microphone. Private, quiet room required; no other people in the room. Test-taker must use an approved device and browser.
LSAT also available at Prometric test centers for candidates who prefer an in-person testing environment. Limited seats per administration; register early to secure preferred location and date.

id_requirements: Government-issued photo ID required. The name on the ID must exactly match the name on the LSAC account. For at-home testing: ID must be held up to the webcam for visual verification.
exam_rules: No personal items, notes, books, printed materials, or scratch paper permitted in the testing area. Digital scratch paper (notes tool) is provided within the testing interface. For at-home: must be alone in a quiet, private room. No virtual machines, dual monitors, or remote desktop software permitted. LSAT Writing must be completed in a separate session using a different proctoring platform (Proctorio). Candidates may pause the exam only between sections. All five LSAT scores from the past 5 years are reported to law schools by default (LSAC policy).
renewal_options: LSAT scores are not certifications requiring renewal. Scores are valid for 5 years from the test date for most ABA-accredited law schools. Some schools may accept scores older than 5 years at their discretion. Candidates may retake the LSAT within lifetime attempt limits.

retake_policy.notes: Candidates may take the LSAT up to 3 times in a single testing year (June 1–May 31), up to 5 times in 5 years, and up to 7 times total over a lifetime. LSAC reports all scores from the last 5 years to law schools. No mandatory waiting period between attempts; candidates may register for the next available administration after receiving scores.

domains.name: Logical Reasoning (argument analysis, flaws, assumptions, strengthening/weakening)
Writing Sample (unscored; argument essay sent directly to law schools)
Analytical Reasoning / Logic Games (ordering, grouping, sequencing) [pre-Aug 2024 format — archived]

official_study_resources.title: Official LSAT Prep on Khan Academy
official_study_resources.notes: Free, adaptive LSAT prep including real LSAT questions, personalized practice, and full-length official practice tests.

official_study_resources.title: LawHub Advantage (Official LSAT Practice)
official_study_resources.notes: $99/year subscription via LawHub. Includes 70+ full-length official LSAT PrepTests, full-length practice tests in the real exam interface, and performance analytics. Some free access available without subscription.

official_study_resources.title: The Official LSAT SuperPrep II
official_study_resources.notes: Includes 3 full PrepTests plus explanations for every question. Available in print and digital formats.
